author: Austin Crawford
title: Marsh-Mucking for Kids
summary: All ages welcome to explore the salt marsh and to learn about the species found at Andy’s Way! (Kids must wear water shoes and parents must attend).
content: All ages welcome to explore the salt marsh and to learn about the species found at Andy’s Way! (Kids must wear water shoes and parents must attend).
address: Andy's Way New Shoreham Washington County

author: Austin Crawford
title: Bake, Book and Bloom
summary: We think you will be delighted with the wonderful offerings from Clark Farms, delicious baked goods (lots of pies), and the interesting assortment of books.
content:
We think you will be delighted with the wonderful offerings from Clark Farms, delicious baked goods (lots of pies), and the interesting assortment of books.

As always, we appreciate your help in getting ready for the big day.  We've already asked for your help with Bake.  For Bloom, the truck from Clark Farms will arrive at the Library at NOON on Friday, May 26.  We need help unloading the truck, then pricing  and organizing the plants on the lawn.  On Saturday, we could use help with tallying purchases and assisting the cashier.

This year, we are only accepting credit cards for Bloom.  We do prefer cash and checks though if possible.

We are so grateful for all your help.  See you soon.

author: Austin Crawford
title: Outdoor Vintage Market
summary: Outdoor Vintage Market at Cottage Chic Decor! Sat June 3rd. Rain date is Sunday June 4th. Interested Vendors please e-mail
content:
Outdoor Vintage Market at Cottage Chic Decor! Sat June 3rd. Rain date is Sunday June 4th. Interested Vendors please e-mail

cottagechicdecor1@gmail.com. 
 

I am an Interior Decorator. I specialize in Cottage/Coastal Chic style and Old World Nautical. Visit my shop for beautiful furniture and home decor. Located at 119 Main Street Wakefield RI.

author: Austin Crawford
title: Block Island Film Festival
summary: The Block Island Film Festival boasts notable actors, directors, screenwriters, filmmakers, student filmmakers and entertainment professionals. A consortium of global industry professionals gather to mingle with contemporaries, and the public, in intimate settings, such as film screenings, spotlight programs, tribute and award ceremonies, question and answer forums, and nightly social mixers.
content:
The Block Island Film Festival boasts notable actors, directors, screenwriters, filmmakers, student filmmakers and entertainment professionals. A consortium of global industry professionals gather to mingle with contemporaries, and the public, in intimate settings, such as film screenings, spotlight programs, tribute and award ceremonies, question and answer forums, and nightly social mixers.

This year’s film festival will operate all programming at the Block Island Maritime Institute in New Harbor, which is located at the edge of the Great Salt Pond. Tickets will be available at the door only. Please arrive at least 15 minutes prior to the beginning of each screening to purchase tickets.

Schedule of events https://blockislandfilmfestival.org/schedule

author: Austin Crawford
title: New trail on the Greenway - James Stover Exploration
summary: Join us for a walk on a new trail to the 2nd highest point on Beacon Hill.  Meet at Greenway entrance on Beacon Hill Road. Contact Kim to sign up email kim.gaffett@tnc.org.
content: Join us for a walk on a new trail to the 2nd highest point on Beacon Hill.  Meet at Greenway entrance on Beacon Hill Road. Contact Kim to sign up email kim.gaffett@tnc.org.

author: Austin Crawford
title: 22 Spring St
summary: Join the Saint Andrews Ladies Guild for their annual Saint Paddy's Day Brunch. This abundant meal of scrambled eggs, corned beef hash, sausage, bacon, hash browned potatoes, scalloped tomatoes, baked beans, Irish oatmeal, fruit salad, home baked breads and muffins and juice, coffee and tea is a community favorite.
content:
Join the Saint Andrews Ladies Guild for their annual Saint Paddy's Day Brunch. This abundant meal of scrambled eggs, corned beef hash, sausage, bacon, hash browned potatoes, scalloped tomatoes, baked beans, Irish oatmeal, fruit salad, home baked breads and muffins and juice, coffee and tea is a community favorite.

 

$12.00 Adults $6.00 Children

author: Austin Crawford
title: 22nd Annual Thanksgiving Beach Walk  To honor the Late Capt. John R. “Rob” Lewis
summary: Meet at Town Beach
content:
Meet at Town Beach

Many of us, when we think of Rob Lewis, Block Island's conservation visionary, remember him striding down Crescent Beach where he walked almost every day, often barefoot, in all kinds of weather. Please help us celebrate this great man, who inspired so many people to donate land and resources to support conservation on this beautiful island. Leashes are required for canine participants. Of course, shoes are optional!

author: Austin Crawford
title: Great Salt Pond Cleanup
summary: Join us as we clean up the Great Salt Pond. To sign up Contact Dee at diandra.verbeystatnc.org must be 18 years or older to participate in water cleanup activities.  All youth 17 years and younger must be accompanied by a parent/legal guardian. The purpose of the cleanup is to haul out marine debris & trash around
content:
Join us as we clean up the Great Salt Pond. To sign up Contact Dee at diandra.verbeystatnc.org must be 18 years or older to participate in water cleanup activities.  All youth 17 years and younger must be accompanied by a parent/legal guardian. The purpose of the cleanup is to haul out marine debris & trash around
New Harbor marinas & GSP coastline. Bring  Closed-toe shoes, water, sunscreen, and gloves (trash bags will be provided).

 

Rain date Monday August 22 9:00am-12:00pm

author: Austin Crawford
title: 2022 Block Island Triathlon
summary: The Island's largest and most popular race begins with a 1/4 mile open water ocean swim; followed by 12 miles of biking over rolling hills with breathtaking views of the island. The last stretch is a 4 mile run on beautiful Cresent Beach. Questions? Call Dave 401-466-3223. email: recreation@new-shoreham.com.
content: The Island's largest and most popular race begins with a 1/4 mile open water ocean swim; followed by 12 miles of biking over rolling hills with breathtaking views of the island. The last stretch is a 4 mile run on beautiful Cresent Beach. Questions? Call Dave 401-466-3223. email: recreation@new-shoreham.com.

author: Austin Crawford
title: Block Island Country Fest
summary: Block Island Country Fest is returning to Ballard’s of Block Island for a sixth consecutive year on July 7, 2022, from 9 a.m. – 7 p.m. A day of music, food, fun, and cocktails Ballard’s Private Beach!  There is NO Cover Charge – This is a FREE Event! So, put on your swimsuit and cowboy boots and head to Ballard’s where you can sip pineapple drink cocktails on Ballard’s Private Beach while listening to country music all day long.
content:
Block Island Country Fest is returning to Ballard’s of Block Island for a sixth consecutive year on July 7, 2022, from 9 a.m. – 7 p.m. A day of music, food, fun, and cocktails Ballard’s Private Beach!  There is NO Cover Charge – This is a FREE Event! So, put on your swimsuit and cowboy boots and head to Ballard’s where you can sip pineapple drink cocktails on Ballard’s Private Beach while listening to country music all day long.
 
The live bands that will be playing are Steve Daggett, Presley & Taylor, Ashley Jordan, Shot Dow, and Chris Ruediger
 
https://ballardsbi.com/country-fest/

author: Austin Crawford
title: Marsh Mucking
